name: Romanée-Conti
description: The most famous vineyard in Burgundy and perhaps the world. A monopole of Domaine de la Romanée-Conti, producing extraordinarily complex and long-lived Pinot Noir.
historicalNotes: The vineyard was owned by the Prince de Conti in the 18th century, giving it its current name.

name: La Tâche
description: Another monopole of DRC, La Tâche produces powerful yet elegant wines with exceptional aging potential.

name: Montrachet
description: Widely considered the greatest white wine vineyard in the world, producing Chardonnay of unparalleled complexity and longevity. Shared between Puligny-Montrachet and Chassagne-Montrachet.
historicalNotes: The name 'Montrachet' comes from 'Mont Rachet' meaning 'bald mountain', referring to its bare hilltop.

name: Chambertin
description: Napoleon's favorite wine, Chambertin produces powerful, structured Pinot Noir with great aging potential.
historicalNotes: Legend says Napoleon insisted on taking Chambertin on his military campaigns.

name: Clos de Vougeot
description: Burgundy's largest Grand Cru, with over 80 different owners. Quality varies significantly depending on the specific parcel location within the clos.
historicalNotes: Originally cultivated by Cistercian monks in the 12th century. The medieval stone wall still surrounds the vineyard.

name: Les Amoureuses
description: Often considered 'Grand Cru quality', Les Amoureuses produces exceptionally elegant and perfumed wines. The name means 'The Lovers'.

name: Clos Saint-Jacques
description: Considered by many to be of Grand Cru quality, Clos Saint-Jacques produces powerful, structured wines with excellent aging potential.
historicalNotes: The climat was owned by the Abbey of Saint-Jacques in the Middle Ages.

name: Les Perrières
description: Meursault's finest Premier Cru, producing structured, mineral Chardonnay with great aging potential. Many consider it worthy of Grand Cru status.
historicalNotes: The name derives from 'perrière' meaning stone quarry, referencing the stony, limestone-rich soil.

name: Bonnes-Mares
description: A Grand Cru straddling Chambolle-Musigny and Morey-Saint-Denis, producing powerful yet elegant wines combining structure and finesse.

name: Corton
description: The largest Grand Cru in Burgundy for red wine, Corton encompasses multiple climats and produces powerful, age-worthy Pinot Noir.
historicalNotes: Named after the Roman Emperor Charlemagne, who owned vineyards on this hill.
